text: Hold thy peace, and come out of him — Jesus rebukes the unclean spirit and casts it out with authority
Let us go into the next towns to preach there also — for this purpose I came forth
I will; be thou clean — Jesus heals the leper with a word of compassion
Tell no one, but show thyself to the priest and offer what Moses commanded for a testimony unto them
Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the tax collector to leave his post and become a disciple
Have ye never read what David did when hungry — entering God's house and eating the shewbread reserved for priests
Stand forth — is it lawful to do good or evil on the sabbath, to save life or to kill — stretch forth thine hand
Who is my mother, or my brethren? Behold my mother and my brethren — those who do God's will are my true family
Know ye not this parable? How then will ye know all parables — understanding the sower unlocks all kingdom teaching
Let us pass over unto the other side — Jesus directs the disciples to cross the sea of Galilee
Peace, be still — Jesus rebukes the wind and commands the sea, and there was a great calm
Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit — what is thy name? Jesus confronts the Gerasene demoniac's legion
Go home to thy friends and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee
Who touched my clothes — Jesus perceives that virtue had gone out of him to heal the woman in the press
Why make ye this ado, and weep? The damsel is not dead, but sleepeth — Jesus rebukes the mourners' unbelief
Talitha cumi — Damsel, I say unto thee, arise — Jesus raises Jairus's daughter by the hand
A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, among his own kin, and in his own house
Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while — Jesus calls the apostles to retreat with him
Give ye them to eat — how many loaves have ye? Go and see — Jesus prepares to feed the five thousand
Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid — Jesus reassures the disciples as he walks on the sea
Laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men — washing pots and cups and many such things
Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand — Jesus calls the multitude to receive his teaching on defilement
If any man have ears to hear, let him hear — Jesus presses the multitude to receive the teaching
Are ye so without understanding? What enters from without cannot defile a man — but that which cometh out defileth him
All these evil things come from within, and defile the man — the heart is the source of moral corruption
Let the children first be filled — it is not meet to take the children's bread and cast it unto the dogs
For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter — Jesus rewards the Syrophoenician's faith
Ephphatha — Be opened — Jesus heals the deaf man with an impeded speech impediment
I have compassion on the multitude — they have been with me three days fasting and would faint by the way home
How many loaves have ye — Jesus asks before feeding the four thousand from seven loaves
Why doth this generation seek after a sign? Verily, no sign shall be given unto this generation
Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod — guard your hearts from their teaching
Why reason ye because ye have no bread — having eyes see ye not, ears hear ye not — how is it ye do not understand
Neither go into the town nor tell it to any in the town — whom do men say that I am
Get thee behind me, Satan — for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men
Elias verily cometh first and restoreth all things — and as it is written, Elias is indeed come and they did unto him as they listed
What question ye with them — Jesus asks the scribes who were disputing with his disciples
O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you, how long shall I suffer you — bring him unto me
How long is it ago since this came unto him — Jesus asks the father of the demoniac boy
If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth — faith opens what unbelief shuts
Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him
This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting — some deliverance requires deeper communion with God
What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way — Jesus exposes the disciples' dispute over greatness
Forbid him not, for none who works a miracle in my name can lightly speak evil of me — he that is not against us is for us
Behold, we go up to Jerusalem — the Son of man shall be delivered, condemned, mocked, scourged, killed, and rise the third day
What would ye that I should do for you — Jesus questions James and John before granting their request
What wilt thou that I should do unto thee — Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole — blind Bartimaeus receives his sight
Go into the village and find a colt tied — loose him and bring him; if any ask, say the Lord hath need of him
No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever — Jesus curses the unfruitful fig tree as a sign against barren religion
Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer — but ye have made it a den of thieves
But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses
I will ask you one question — answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things — was John's baptism from heaven or men
Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things — Jesus refuses the chief priests' challenge
Thou art not far from the kingdom — how say the scribes Christ is David's son when David himself by the Spirit calls him Lord
Seest thou these great buildings — there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down
Let her alone — she hath wrought a good work on me, anointing my body for burial; this shall be told for a memorial of her
Go into the city and follow a man bearing a pitcher of water — say the Master asks where the guestchamber is, and prepare the passover
Verily I say unto you, one of you which eateth with me shall betray me — Jesus foretells the betrayal at the table
It is one of the twelve that dippeth with me in the dish — woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed; better never born
All ye shall be offended this night — I will smite the shepherd and the sheep shall be scattered — but after I am risen I will go before you into Galilee
Verily I say unto thee, this very night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice
Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves — I was daily teaching in the temple, but the scriptures must be fulfilled
Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice — Peter remembers the word and weeps bitterly
Thou sayest it — Jesus answers Pilate concerning being the King of the Jews
